channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Republicans, Democrats preparing for legal battles over election
item.description: Dozens of lawsuits that could set the stage for challenges after the votes are counted are already playing out in courts across the country.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, COLLEEN LONG and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris attends church in Georgia and encourages Black congregants to vote
item.description: Kamala Harris has gone to church in an Atlanta suburb, addressing the faithful and encouraging Black congregants to vote as part of a nationwide campaign push known as “souls to the polls.”.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump talks about Arnold Palmer's genitalia at Pennsylvania rally
item.description: Politicians saluting Palmer in his hometown is nothing new. Trump spent 12 full minutes doing so at the top of his speech in Latrobe on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats fight for Tester in Montana as Sheehy pulls away
item.description: Democrats are refusing to give up on Sen. Jon Tester’s (D-Mont.) reelection bid even as his chances appear bleak with just more than two weeks until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A former Park Service ranger said Friday that U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan — going public with an accusation that has nagged the Republican’s campaign for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Hovde shines spotlight on Tammy Baldwin's Wall Street partner during Wisconsin debate
item.description: Eric Hovde hammered Sen. Baldwin for not disclosing her longtime partner's finances, pointing out her position as a Wall Street wealth adviser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll finds majority of Americans say they're worse off than four years ago, similar to 1992 election
item.description: A Gallup poll suggested that the majority of Americans believe they are worse off than they were four years ago, citing economic strain as a top issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky Tiktok suit says company considered using KY creators to sway Sen. Mitch McConnell
item.description: The suit says videos by Kentucky small businesses were identified as possibly of interest to the senator, who has expressed concern about the app's effect on kids
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman, MPR News

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Ag leaders voice concerns over negative impacts of tariffs proposed in presidential election
item.description: Midwestern agricultural leaders say they are concerned new tariffs being suggested by former President Donald Trump as part of his reelection bid could result in a damaging trade war. They say it could cost the corn and soybeans billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC
